# References

This page is the authoritative bibliography for the AEGIS Constitution.

References are divided into two categories:

1. **Normative References** — directly cited in constitutional articles and specifications.
2. **Background Reading** — intellectual influences informing the architecture but not required to justify specific clauses.

Each entry is assigned a stable identifier (`R01`, `R02`, …).  
Footnotes in canonical documents link to these identifiers.

---

# I. Normative References (Cited in Constitution)

---

## R01 — NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)

- NIST overview: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework  
- PDF (NIST AI 100-1): https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/nist.ai.100-1.pdf  

---

## R02 — ISO/IEC 23894:2023 (AI Risk Management Guidance)

- ISO catalog entry: https://www.iso.org/standard/77304.html  

---

## R03 — Saltzer & Schroeder (Least Privilege and Protection Principles)

- Original paper (SS75): https://www.cs.umd.edu/~jkatz/security/downloads/SS75.pdf  

---

## R04 — Madison, Federalist No. 51 (Separation of Powers / Checks and Balances)

- Avalon Project (Yale Law School):  
  https://avalon.law.yale.edu/18th_century/fed51.asp  

---

## R05 — Ogata, Modern Control Engineering (Control Feedback Concepts)

- Pearson listing:  
  https://www.pearson.com/en-us/subject-catalog/p/modern-control-engineering/P200000003521/9780137551064  

---

## R06 — Perrow, Normal Accidents (High-Risk Technologies)

- Princeton University Press listing:  
  https://press.princeton.edu/books/paperback/9780691004129/normal-accidents  

---

## R07 — Leveson, Engineering a Safer World (Systems Thinking / Safety)

- MIT Press listing:  
  https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262533690/engineering-a-safer-world/  

---

## R08 — CISA Secure by Design

- https://www.cisa.gov/securebydesign  

---

## R09 — NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 (Separation of Duties / AC-5)

- PDF: https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/SpecialPublications/NIST.SP.800-53r5.pdf  
- AC-5 summary: https://csf.tools/reference/nist-sp-800-53/r5/ac/ac-5/  

---

# II. Background Reading (Intellectual Context)

The following works inform the philosophical and risk-theoretic framing of AEGIS but are not directly cited in constitutional clauses.

---

## R10 — Amodei et al., Concrete Problems in AI Safety

- arXiv abstract: https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.06565  
- PDF: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1606.06565  

---

## R11 — Russell, Human Compatible (AI Control Problem)

- Publisher listing:  
  https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/566677/human-compatible-by-stuart-russell/  

---

## R12 — Locke, Second Treatise of Government

- Project Gutenberg: https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/7370  

---

## R13 — Montesquieu, The Spirit of Laws

- Liberty Fund edition:  
  https://oll.libertyfund.org/title/montesquieu-complete-works-vol-1-the-spirit-of-laws  

---

## R14 — Taleb, The Black Swan

- Publisher listing:  
  https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/176226/the-black-swan-second-edition-by-nassim-nicholas-taleb/
